system:
You are the Watchman of a legal AI lighthouse. Documents arrive in the watched folder; you decide what happens next. You read the filename and the first ~1500 characters, nothing more. Your job is triage, not analysis.

Output a single JSON object with EXACTLY these fields:

{
  "documentType": "jv" | "nda" | "employment" | "lease" | "loan" | "saas" | "policy" | "other",
  "jurisdiction": "string (best guess from defined-terms, governing-law clauses, party addresses; empty string if unclear)",

CLIENT: Acme Holdings (investment, NSW)
CLIENT CONCERNS: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
FIRST 1500 CHARS OF DOCUMENT:
Exhibit 2.01   Strategic Alliance Agreement   This agreement is made and entered into this 30th day of November, 2017 by and between Bravatek Solutions, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Colorado, ("Bravatek"), with an address at 2028 E. Ben White Blvd., Unit #240-2835, Austin, Texas, 78741, and Sibannac, Inc. ("COMPANY"), a corporation organized under the laws of Nevada, with an address at 2122 E Highland Avenue, Suite 425, Phoenix, Arizona 85016.   Whereas, Bravatek is a corporation, which has technical expertise in security-related software, tools and systems/services (including telecom services) to support, deploy and test its current and potential customers' most critical initiatives.   Whereas, COMPANY a corporation engaged in the business of providing a novel, patented Air Cylinder Wheel, to replace the need for conventional rubber tires on a large range of vehicles used in the mining, military, construction and industries, delivering cost savings and reducing adverse environmental impact.   Whereas, the parties desire to enter into a business relationship which will designate Bravatek as the project based business partnership channel for governmental and non-governmental departments / agencies / units for the purpose of promoting COMPANY's relevant capabilities, products and/or service solutions. Now therefore, the parties mutually agree to enter into a strategic alliance under the following terms and conditions:   1) Duties of Bravatek   Br
Return your JSON triage decision now.

CLIENT: Acme Holdings (investment, NSW)
CLIENT'S CONCERNS: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
DOCUMENT TYPE: jv
CLIENT'S ROLE: risk-conscious participant

PRIOR FIRM POSITIONS ON SIMILAR CLAUSES (advisory — the live document outranks):
- [TENTATIVE] [PREC-ad09177d] Joint Several Liability Pattern — Lack of clarity on joint and several liability and capital call mechanics could lead to disputes and delays in project execution. (effectiveness 20%, seen 1x)
[CONFIRMED] = the firm has settled on this position across multiple matters; weight it heavily unless the document's language plainly contradicts.
[TENTATIVE] = the firm has flagged this pattern but not confirmed; treat as a hypothesis to test against the document.
Reconcile your judgment with the firm's prior positions where the document supports it. If the document contains language INCONSISTENT with a prior position, trust the document and explain the divergence in your concern.

=== CLAUSE 2: Duties ===
2) Duties of COMPANY   COMPANY agrees to use its best efforts to promote and support project lead finding and after-sales support of Bravatek by:   a. Listing Bravatek in all appropriate sales and marketing materials as a non-exclusive alliance partner (with focus of government customers)   b. Provide timely responses to both technical and administrative questions posed by Bravatek.   c. Promote Bravatek's product and service offerings whenever possible.   d. Aid Bravatek in the writing of any technical/marketing/sales documents when requested and participate in mutually-agreed upon sales calls.   e. Provide Bravatek with co-branded marketing material that can be emailed or handed to prospective clients.
=== END CLAUSE 2 ===

4) Compensation   When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project.

 2







  For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.

5) Confidentiality   "Confidential information" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. The obligation of non-disclosure shall not apply to the following:   a. Information at or after such time that is publicly available through no fault of either party   b. === CLIENT STANCE (use ONLY for risk framing — NOT as document facts) ===
Company name: Acme Holdings
Client's industry: investment
Client's home jurisdiction: NSW
Client's stated concerns: joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs
NOTE: the client may or may not be a party to this document. Do not name the client as a party unless the per-clause findings below explicitly say so.

=== PER-CLAUSE FINDINGS FROM THE TEAM (the source of truth for parties, jurisdiction, subject matter) ===
cl 2 (Duties) — favours non-operator
  summary: This clause outlines the non-operator's responsibilities in supporting Bravatek, but lacks clarity on financial obligations and potential liabilities.

cl 3 (Obligations) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines joint and several liability for the JV partners, particularly in terms of product development and marketing efforts. This could lead to disputes and delays if not clearly defined.
  [major] Joint and several liability is not clearly defined, particularly in the context of financial obligations and dispute resolution mechanisms. This could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

cl 4 (Compensation) — favours operator
  summary: This clause outlines the compensation structure for sales generated by Bravatek, but lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs, and potential disputes regarding project revenue.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs, and potential disputes regarding project revenue.

cl 5 (Confidentiality) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause defines 'Confidential Information' and outlines non-disclosure obligations for the parties. However, it lacks clarity on how disputes regarding confidentiality will be handled.
  [minor] The clause lacks clarity on how disputes regarding confidentiality will be handled.

cl 6 (Term) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines confidentiality obligations for a defined period after termination, which could be relevant to dispute resolution or future business dealings.
  [minor] The clause defines a confidentiality period of five years after termination. This could lead to disputes if the parties disagree on what constitutes confidential information or how long it should remain confidential.

cl 7 (Term) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause outlines a standard twelve-month term with automatic renewal, and termination provisions. However, the lack of specific details on capital calls, joint and several liability, and indemnification could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on capital call mechanics and joint and several liability, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
  [major] The clause does not specify the mechanism for capital calls or how non-operator participation will be handled during such events. This lack of clarity could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
  [major] The clause does not specify the mechanism for capital calls or how non-operator participation will be handled during such events. This lack of clarity could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

cl 8 (Notices) — favours neutral
  summary: The clause defines notice delivery addresses, but lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, and indemnification carve-outs, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
  [major] The clause lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, and indemnification carve-outs, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

cl 10 (Indemnification) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause establishes joint and several indemnification for both parties, potentially leading to disputes over liability allocation and cost recovery.
  [major] This clause establishes joint and several indemnification for both parties, potentially leading to disputes over liability allocation and cost recovery.

cl 11 (Modifications) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause establishes a clear requirement for all modifications to the agreement to be in writing and signed by all parties, minimizing potential disputes arising from informal changes.

cl 12 (Assignment) — favours operator
  summary: This clause limits assignment rights, potentially hindering flexibility and increasing risk for the non-operator if unforeseen circumstances arise.
  [major] The clause restricts assignment rights, which could hinder flexibility and increase risk for the non-operator in case of unforeseen circumstances. 

cl 13 (Entire Agreement) — favours neutral
  summary: This clause asserts the complete agreement between parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations or agreements. This could potentially limit negotiation room for future disputes.
  [major] This clause asserts the complete agreement between parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations or agreements. This could potentially limit negotiation room for future disputes.

Listing Bravatek in all appropriate sales and marketing materials as a non-exclusive alliance partner (with focus of government customers)   b. Provide timely responses to both technical and administrative questions posed by Bravatek.   c. Promote Bravatek's product and service offerings whenever possible.   d. Aid Bravatek in the writing of any technical/marketing/sales documents when requested and participate in mutually-agreed upon sales calls.   e. Provide Bravatek with co-branded marketing material that can be emailed or handed to prospective clients.
clauses.concern: This clause lacks clarity on the non-operator's financial obligations and potential liabilities for supporting Bravatek, particularly regarding the scope of support and any associated costs.

clauses.title: 3. Obligations
clauses.text: Bravatek and COMPANY agree to jointly: a. Develop and implement a joint Product Solution and Application Strategy whereby targeted markets/potential client- types/applications are mutually agreed upon;   b. Support each other in all agreed-upon technical, marketing and promotional efforts;   c. Develop a joint strategy for developing new product/services/capabilities to mutually benefit both parties;   d. Utilize each other as Preferred Vendors for services whenever possible upon mutual agreement.
clauses.concern: Joint and several liability is not clearly defined, particularly in the context of financial obligations and dispute resolution mechanisms. This could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

clauses.title: 4. Compensation
clauses.text: When custom Products are designed, developed and to be delivered to Bravatek-identified perspective clients, the parties shall agree to a proposed sales price for use during the project in writing prior to the commencement of each project. For any Product or Solution sold to any perspective clients introduced by Bravatek registered with COMPANY via email to COMPANY's CEO and delivered through Bravatek or a COMPANY-designated distribution affiliate(s) or sales channel(s), Bravatek will receive a lead-finder fee, to be mutually discussed and finally decided by COMPANY at the range of minimum of 10% to maximum of 20% of project revenue, with an exact fee to be depending upon the overall project sales margin and cost of development and delivery of each project, payable NET 30 days after each client payment on delivered products received at COMPANY's bank account.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, governing law, penalty / liquidated damages, indemnification carve-outs, and potential disputes regarding project revenue.

clauses.title: 5. Confidentiality
clauses.text: ‘Confidential information’ shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information, documents and materials related to client projects of party and products, services and business of each of the parties. COMPANY and Bravatek agree to maintain in strict confidence and not to disclose or disseminate, or to use for any purposes other than performance of the projects, the Confidential Information disclosed.  The obligation of non-disclosure shall not apply to the following:   a. Information at or after such time that is publicly available through no fault of either party   b. Information at or after such time that is disclosed to either party by a third party entitled to disclose such information   c. Information which is required by law to be disclosed to federal, state or local authorities.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on how disputes regarding confidentiality will be handled.

clauses.title: 6. Term
clauses.text: For a period of five (5) years after termination of this Agreement, the parties shall treat as confidential all information and take every reasonable precaution and use all reasonable efforts to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of the same. The parties agree to take all steps reasonably necessary and appropriate to ensure that their employees, agents, and/or assistants treat all information as confidential and to ensure that such employees, agents, and/or assistants are familiar with and abide by the terms of this Agreement.
clauses.concern: The clause defines a confidentiality period of five years after termination. This could lead to disputes if the parties disagree on what constitutes confidential information or how long it should remain confidential.

clauses.title: 7. Term
clauses.text: The term of this Agreement is twelve (12) months from the date hereof, and will be automatically renewed for one (1) additional twelve month period unless either party shall notify the other in writing of its intention not to renew. Such notice must be given ninety (90) days prior to expiration of the original term. This Agreement may also be terminated by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice.
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on capital call mechanics and joint and several liability, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

clauses.title: 8. Notices
clauses.text: Any notices required under this Agreement shall be delivered to:   Bravatek Technologies, Inc. 2028 E. Ben White Blvd., Unit #240-2835 Austin, Texas 78741   Sibannac, Inc. 2122 E Highland Avenue, STE 425 Phoenix, AZ 85016
clauses.concern: The clause lacks clarity on joint and several liability, capital call mechanics, and indemnification carve-outs, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

clauses.title: 10. Indemnification
clauses.text: COMPANY shall indemnify Bravatek, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of COMPANY or breach of COMPANY of any of its obligations under this Agreement.  Bravatek shall indemnify COMPANY, its directors, officers and employees, for any and all damages, costs, expenses, and other liabilities, including reasonable attorney's fees and court costs, incurred in connection with any third-party claim, action or proceeding arising from the negligence or intentional misconduct of Bravatek or breach of Bravatek of any of its obligations under this Agreement.
clauses.concern: This clause establishes joint and several indemnification for both parties, potentially leading to disputes over liability allocation and cost recovery.

clauses.title: 11. Modifications
clauses.text: No changes or modifications of this Agreement or any of its terms shall be deemed effective unless in writing and executed by the parties hereto.
clauses.concern: This clause establishes a clear requirement for all modifications to the agreement to be in writing and signed by all parties, minimizing potential disputes arising from informal changes.

clauses.title: 12. Assignment
clauses.text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other party.
clauses.concern: The clause restricts assignment rights, which could hinder flexibility and increase risk for the non-operator in case of unforeseen circumstances.

clauses.title: 13. Entire Agreement
clauses.text: This Agreement represents the complete and entire understanding between the parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, or agreements, either written or oral, regarding this subject matter. This Agreement shall not be considered accepted, approved or otherwise effective until signed by the appropriate parties.
clauses.concern: This clause asserts the complete agreement between parties and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations or agreements. This could potentially limit negotiation room for future disputes.

risks.description: Lack of clarity on financial obligations, potential liabilities, dispute resolution mechanisms, capital calls, and indemnification carve-outs could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
risks.citation: cl 2, cl 3, cl 4, cl 10, cl 11, cl 12, cl 7

risks.description: Joint and several liability for the JV partners is not clearly defined, particularly in the context of financial obligations and dispute resolution mechanisms. This could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.
The clause lacks clarity on capital call mechanics and joint and several liability, which could lead to disputes and delays in project execution.

recommendations: Issue a formal request for clarification on the financial obligations, potential liabilities, dispute resolution mechanisms, capital call mechanics, and indemnification carve-outs outlined in the agreement.
Demand that Bravatek and Sibannac engage in good faith negotiations to clarify these points before proceeding with the joint venture.
Audit the existing legal framework for joint ventures to ensure it aligns with the specific needs of this project.
confidenceNote: This document was analysed entirely on-device using a local model. No content was transmitted externally. For complex legal matters, verify findings with qualified counsel.
